reasoning: Pgm3 encodes phosphoglucomutase 3, a key enzyme in the hexosamine biosynthetic pathway that generates UDP‑GlcNAc for protein N‑glycosylation and GPI‑anchor synthesis. CRISPRi‑mediated knockdown of Pgm3 lowers cellular UDP‑GlcNAc levels, leading to defective N‑glycosylation of nascent secretory proteins. Improperly glycosylated proteins accumulate in the endoplasmic reticulum, activating the unfolded protein response (UPR). Hsp90b1 (GRP94) is an ER‑resident Hsp90 chaperone whose transcription is strongly induced as part of the UPR to assist folding of misfolded proteins. Therefore, reducing Pgm3 activity is expected to cause ER stress and consequently up‑regulate Hsp90b1 expression.
